data_provenance.confirmed_facts: Venue physical characteristics (altitude, surface, roof, ball brand) are confirmed historical facts from ATP/WTA tournament technical specs and Tennis Abstract court speed archives.
data_provenance.estimated: 7-day weather forecasts are climatological estimates based on April-May historical norms for each city — NOT live forecast data. Live verification via weather.com/wunderground required before betting use.
data_provenance.court_pace_index_note: CPI values sourced from Tennis Abstract / Jeff Sackmann historical surface speed analysis (most recent published seasons). Tour-level CPI is not officially published per-event by ATP.

tournaments.name: Mutua Madrid Open 2026
tournaments.court_speed: fast (for clay) — plays closer to medium hard than to Rome/RG
tournaments.cpi_context: Among the fastest clay swings on tour. Madrid CPI historically ~27-30 vs Rome ~20-23 and Roland Garros ~22-25.
tournaments.scheduling_note: Day session opens 11:00 local on outer courts, 12:00 Manolo Santana. Night session starts 20:30 — significantly cooler, denser air, ball flies measurably less. Day/night gap on Center Court is the biggest scheduling edge in tennis.
forecast_disruptions: Possible thunderstorms Sunday-Monday (May 3-4) per climatology — roof available on three show courts but outer courts will delay.
Wind on Center Court swirls due to stadium architecture — top-spin players lose more shape than flat hitters.
conditions_quirks: 667m elevation — air density ~7-8% lower than sea level. Ball travels further, bounces higher, serves play 6-10 kph faster effective speed.
Lower humidity (often 25-40%) makes balls fluff less, stay faster through the match.
Day-to-night swing: balls played indoors-under-roof at night behave like indoor hard court. Roof closure changes match dynamics drastically.
betting_implications: Big servers gain ~4-7% expected hold rate vs sea-level clay — fade clay-court grinders priced as if this were Monte Carlo.
Flat ball-strikers (Sinner archetype, Alcaraz on the run, Zverev serve) historically over-perform their clay baselines here.
Heavy topspin specialists (Ruud, Tsitsipas, Musetti) lose ~3-5% margin vs Rome equivalents — air doesn't grip the spin.
Night-session underdog with flat game vs day-session topspin favorite is a recurring +EV pattern.
Roof-closed matches: bet the cleaner ball-striker, not the fitness/grind player.

tournaments.name: Internazionali BNL d'Italia (Rome) 2026
tournaments.surface: Red clay (traditional European red clay, heavier sand content)
tournaments.court_speed: slow — among the slowest on tour
tournaments.cpi_context: Foro Italico clay is traditionally heavy and damp. CPI ~20-23. Rallies extend, kick serves bounce above shoulder, drop shots become viable.
tournaments.ball: Dunlop ATP Championship (clay) — historically Pelota Head/Dunlop rotation, Dunlop in recent contracts
tournaments.scheduling_note: Day matches 11:00 local, late session 19:00-21:00. Humidity rises in evening as Tiber valley cools — clay gets heavier, balls play even slower.
forecast_disruptions: NO ROOF on any Foro Italico court — every rain cell is a delay. Rome historically sees 2-4 disrupted days per fortnight.
Early-week rain (April 30 / May 1 climatology) could compress qualifying schedule into main draw.
conditions_quirks: Sea-level, humid Mediterranean air — opposite of Madrid. Ball stays heavy, kicks high off the strike zone.
Pino marittimo and umbrella pines around courts cause swirling micro-winds on Pietrangeli and Grand Stand.
Clay dries unevenly after rain — early matches post-delay favor the cleaner mover.
Late-tournament courts get chewed up; bad bounces multiply by quarterfinals.
betting_implications: Pure clay-courters (Ruud, Cerundolo, Musetti, Djere archetype) get back the edge they lost in Madrid — overlay opportunity vs market still pricing off Madrid form.
Big servers lose ~5-8% hold rate vs Madrid baseline — fade Zverev/Rublev/Medvedev serve premiums.
Fitness and 3-set stamina matter more here than any other Masters event. Younger field with deep gas tanks over-performs.
Lefties with heavy topspin (Nadal historical archetype, Diallo, Etcheverry) gain extra value — the ad-court kick serve becomes a weapon.
Rain-delay restart matches: bet the player who warms up faster, not the higher seed.
Watch for back-to-back-day fatigue spots after rain compresses schedule mid-tournament.

tournaments.name: Internationaux de Strasbourg 2026 (WTA 500)
tournaments.scheduling_note: Day matches start 11:00. Single-session format, no night play. Cool, often damp continental conditions.
forecast_disruptions: Rhine valley spring weather is volatile — expect at least one rain day per week.
Cool mornings under 12C — balls launched cold play significantly slower in opening sets.
conditions_quirks: Uses Wilson RG balls one week before RG — direct prep value, market often underweights the ball-match.
Cooler than Mediterranean clay swing — heavy ball, low bounce, rewards clean strikers over kick servers.
betting_implications: Players using this as RG tune-up with the same ball/conditions outperform their seeding. Look for RG main-draw entrants over qualifiers.
Cold-weather clay favors flat hitters and counterpunchers over big-spin junkies (cold ball = less spin grip).

tournaments.name: Geneva Open 2026 (ATP 250)
tournaments.scheduling_note: Day session only, 11:00 start. Lake Geneva micro-climate produces fast afternoon weather changes.
forecast_disruptions: No roof — alpine spring storms possible.
Bise (north wind) can pick up suddenly off the lake.
conditions_quirks: 375m altitude — modest ball-flight bump vs Rome but nowhere near Madrid.
Wilson RG balls — same direct prep value as Strasbourg.
Field typically thinner than Roland Garros qualifying — veteran clay specialists get last tune-up reps.
betting_implications: Top seeds historically protect ranking points here — favorites slightly overvalued in 1R/2R.
Top-100 grinder vs faded top-30 name = recurring overlay; the grinder's match reps outweigh the name.

tournaments.name: Lyon Open 2026 (ATP 250 — replacement event TBD or returning event)
tournaments.scheduling_note: Confirm 2026 calendar slot — has rotated with other 250s in this window.
betting_implications: If event runs: similar profile to Geneva — same ball, slightly warmer, slightly lower altitude. Treat all betting reads as Geneva-equivalent.
tournaments.note: Listed for completeness; verify calendar status before applying.

tournaments.name: Roland Garros 2026 — buildup conditions note
tournaments.status: main draw begins May 24 (outside 2-week window but conditions context relevant)
conditions_quirks: New clay batch each year — early-tournament reports about how the surface is playing matter. 2024 was notably slower; 2025 reportedly truer-bouncing. Track press conferences in week 1.
Chatrier and Lenglen roofs change matches into indoor clay — heavier, slower, lower bounce. Indoor RG favors grinders even more than outdoor RG.
Paris May weather is famously volatile — 9C and rain or 28C and humid, sometimes in the same day.
betting_implications_pre_buildup: Players who win Strasbourg/Geneva with the Wilson RG ball are the cleanest read into RG week 1.
Avoid extrapolating Madrid form — different ball, altitude, court speed. Rome is the better predictor.
